 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-oy5","title":"fcp.sentry: Alert rule configuration and management","description":"# fcp.sentry: Alert rule configuration and management (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide controlled access to Sentry alert rule configuration so agents can help maintain:
- issue alerts
- performance alerts
- notification routing

This is **dangerous** (high blast radius). Must be approval-gated by policy.

## Scope
### Read
- List alert rules (project/org scope where available).
- Get rule definition + current enabled/disabled state.

### Write (dangerous)
- Create/update/delete alert rules.
- Enable/disable rule.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.alerts.read`
- `sentry.alerts.write` (dangerous; requires approval)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For any write:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
    - rule id
    - structural hash of rule definition
    - correlation id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.alert.list`
  - `sentry.alert.get`
  - `sentry.alert.create`
  - `sentry.alert.update`
  - `sentry.alert.enable`
  - `sentry.alert.disable`
  - `sentry.alert.delete`
- Safe rule templates:
  - avoid arbitrary code/script injection
  - prefer constrained rule DSL + parameterization

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - CRUD flows
  - validation failures
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Alert rule changes are mechanically gated, auditable, and reversible.

Support for issue/metric/crash/custom alert types with condition/action/filter chains. 145 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:31:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-oz1v","title":"TEST: Rate Limiting Unit Tests (ThrottleViolation, Backpressure, Quota Enforcement)","description":"# TEST: Rate Limiting Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate rate limiting / backpressure behaviors (quota, throttling, time-bounds) are correct and produce explainable denials.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for rate limiting and quota enforcement.

## Test Categories

### ThrottleViolation Detection
- [ ] Requests exceeding rate produce ThrottleViolation
- [ ] Violation includes retry_after_ms
- [ ] Violation includes quota details
- [ ] Violation is logged to audit

### Backpressure Signals
- [ ] Backpressure signal sent when approaching limit
- [ ] Client respects backpressure hint
- [ ] Gradual backoff before hard rejection
- [ ] Backpressure clears when load drops

### Quota Enforcement
- [ ] Per-connector quotas enforced
- [ ] Per-zone quotas enforced
- [ ] Per-operation quotas enforced
- [ ] Quota reset on time window
- [ ] Quota pooling across operations

### Burst Handling
- [ ] Token bucket allows bursts
- [ ] Burst capacity is configurable
- [ ] Burst doesn't exceed hard limits
- [ ] Burst refill rate is correct

### Distributed Rate Limiting
- [ ] Quotas shared across mesh nodes
- [ ] Quota sync is eventually consistent
- [ ] No double-spending on quota
- [ ] Partition handling for quotas

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/rate_limiting/`
- `throttle_violation.cbor`
- `backpressure_signal.cbor`
- `quota_state.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_quota_enforcement",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "connector_id": "...",
  "quota_used": 95,
  "quota_limit": 100,
  "throttled": false,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rate limits are enforced correctly
- Quotas prevent resource exhaustion
- Audit trail for all throttle events

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","title":"[MOR/C3.5] Change default simulate() to deny; fix default-deny violation","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:57.765589Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:36:36.489235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T21:51:37.610429Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","default-deny","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:07.503486Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2644,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Change the default implementation of simulate() in the FcpConnector trait from returning SimulateResponse::allowed(req.id) to returning SimulateResponse::denied(req.id, reason: operation_not_simulated). WHY: J1 Deontic found this is a DIRECT VIOLATION of the default-deny principle. A connector that does not override simulate() currently implicitly claims ALL operations are allowed. This means a new connector scaffold that ships without implementing simulate() silently tells the system it can handle anything. The correct default is: deny everything the connector has not explicitly declared it can simulate. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/connector.rs or crates/fcp-sdk/src/lib.rs — the default impl of simulate() in the FcpConnector trait. Also update fwc new_cmd.rs scaffold template to include a simulate() override with proper operation matching. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Default simulate() returns denied. (2) Existing connectors that rely on default must be updated (or explicitly override with allowed for their operations). (3) fwc new scaffold includes simulate() override template. (4) At least 3 tests: default returns denied, override returns allowed, denied includes reason code. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day for the default change + 0.5 day per connector that needs updating. Quick audit needed: how many connectors rely on the default simulate()? DESIGN NOTE: This is a one-line change with outsized security impact. It converts simulate() from opt-out safety (dangerous default) to opt-in safety (safe default). Any connector that was relying on the old default was ALREADY WRONG — it was claiming support for operations it had not validated.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:50Z"},{"id":2652,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified complete: default simulate() returns SimulateResponse::denied(FCP-3010) since connector.rs:81-89. Scaffold template in new_cmd.rs:2126 has full simulate() override with operation validation, state checks, and capability verification. protocol.rs has 3+ SimulateResponse tests (allowed, denied with reason/code, with_cost). 44 connectors override simulate() explicitly; remaining ~46 correctly use default-deny. All acceptance criteria met.","created_at":"2026-04-10T21:51:16Z"},{"id":2653,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Changed default simulate() from allowed→denied (FCP-3010). Fixed pre-existing phantom type compile errors. 4 new tests, 3898 lib tests pass, clippy clean. Commit 0ce30890.","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:02:45Z"},{"id":2657,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.5 Default simulate() to deny — already implemented. FcpConnector::simulate() returns SimulateResponse::denied('default deny — override simulate() to provide dry-run capability') as verified in connector.rs. No changes needed.","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:36:36Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-pcmm8","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-registry supply-chain config enforcement is trivially bypassable","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1679-1708 (commit 0f14b9c9 enforce supply-chain verification config) — enforce_supply_chain_verification_config treats TUF required and Sigstore required as satisfied whenever ANY SupplyChainEvidence is passed (even a default one with transparency_log_present=false and empty attestations). SupplyChainEvidence has no tuf_verified or sigstore_verified fields (crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:290), so the config-level require_tuf/require_sigstore guards are presence-only and give operators a false sense of enforcement. Suggested fix: add verified flags to SupplyChainEvidence, populated ONLY by real verifier adapters; require those bools to be true when required. Until then, consider failing these branches with a runtime Internal error until real verification wires up, so operators do not deploy with require_tuf=true thinking they are protected. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:02:16.384406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:27.229155Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-pcmm8","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-registry supply-chain config enforcement is trivially bypassable","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1679-1708 (commit 0f14b9c9 enforce supply-chain verification config) — enforce_supply_chain_verification_config treats TUF required and Sigstore required as satisfied whenever ANY SupplyChainEvidence is passed (even a default one with transparency_log_present=false and empty attestations). SupplyChainEvidence has no tuf_verified or sigstore_verified fields (crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:290), so the config-level require_tuf/require_sigstore guards are presence-only and give operators a false sense of enforcement. Suggested fix: add verified flags to SupplyChainEvidence, populated ONLY by real verifier adapters; require those bools to be true when required. Until then, consider failing these branches with a runtime Internal error until real verification wires up, so operators do not deploy with require_tuf=true thinking they are protected. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:02:16.384406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:48.353936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:48.352678Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2826,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pcmm8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit c046efde.

**Fix summary**:
- SupplyChainEvidence gains tuf_verified + sigstore_verified booleans (populated only by real verifier adapters).
- enforce_supply_chain_verification_config now requires those bools to be true, not merely evidence.is_some().
- fwc production sites (build_registry_supply_chain_evidence in supply_chain_cmd.rs, RegistrySupplyChainEvidence in main.rs) carry both at false with explicit SECURITY comments — those paths only validate publisher signatures, so deployments with require_tuf/require_sigstore will now correctly fail-closed there until real adapter wiring lands (matching the reviewer's intent).
- 34 struct-literal construction sites patched (compile-driven).

**Regression tests** added to the ::tests module:
- verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_tuf_evidence — asserts unverified evidence rejected + positive control (tuf_verified=true satisfies gate)
- verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_sigstore_evidence — same for sigstore

**Verification**:
- cargo check -p fcp-registry -p fwc --all-targets: clean
- cargo test -p fcp-registry --lib: 354 pass (was 352 + 2 new), 6 pre-existing fail (mock_registry_*, metamorphic_install_verify — ObjectStore::AlreadyExists + manifest_signing_bytes unrelated)
- cargo test -p fcp-registry --features test-mocks --test no_mock_integration: 49 pass, 39 pre-existing fail (unrelated manifest validation rule for operation capability declaration). Confirmed baseline-equivalent via stash-and-rerun.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pdgc","title":"TEST: fcp-config Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-config Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for configuration validation and schema framework.

## Test Categories

### Schema Validation
- [ ] JSON Schema validation
- [ ] Required field enforcement
- [ ] Type validation
- [ ] Format validation (URL, email, etc.)
- [ ] Custom validators

### Configuration Loading
- [ ] TOML parsing
- [ ] JSON parsing
- [ ] Environment variable expansion
- [ ] File path resolution
- [ ] Default value handling

### Secret Reference Resolution
- [ ] Secret placeholder parsing
- [ ] Vault integration mocking
- [ ] Environment variable secrets
- [ ] File-based secrets

### Validation Errors
- [ ] Clear error messages
- [ ] Path to invalid field
- [ ] Suggested fixes
- [ ] Multiple error collection

### Configuration Merging
- [ ] Override precedence
- [ ] Deep merge behavior
- [ ] Array handling

## Coverage Target: >95%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:48.109383666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:34.771665015Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:34.771665015Z","close_reason":"Superseded - parent bead fcp-config is closed. Config testing is now part of manifest testing (1n78.27.1).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pdgc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-5y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pgtxo","title":"[fcp-host] Verify capability token signatures in admin_state","description":"verify_capability_token() currently hardcodes signature_valid=false even for host-issued tokens, which contradicts the method contract and the existing tests. Persist issuer verifying keys by key ID and use them to perform real COSE signature verification before computing valid/signature_valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T01:17:48.662158Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T01:44:52.212752Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T01:44:52.212553Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pizld","title":"Promote OAuth CSRF+state+redirect helpers to fcp-oauth shared surface; unblocked by conformance harness.rs:861","description":"Follow-up from swarm audit 2026-04-19. Github connector has scope-limited fix; broader connector OAuth helpers should migrate once the conformance harness blocker at crates/fcp-conformance/src/harness.rs:861 is resolved.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BeigeCave","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:45:33.342440Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:25:54.258541Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:25:54.258014Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2781,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pizld","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: releasing claim — collision with parallel in-flight work. fcp-oauth/src/redirect.rs (validate_registered_redirect_uri / validate_callback_redirect_uri / is_allowlisted / ensure_registered_redirect_allowlisted, plus OAuthError::InvalidRedirectUri + RedirectUriNotAllowlisted variants) is uncommitted but already in another agent's tree, while connectors/github/src/connector.rs has an uncommitted diff that imports ensure_allowlisted_redirect_uri / ensure_callback_redirect_is_allowlisted / normalize_registered_redirect_uri (different API). Whoever finishes pizld needs to pick one API and reconcile. Dropping back to open. Also: I created crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs as a parallel artifact before noticing redirect.rs existed — it is NOT wired into lib.rs (no mod declaration), so it does not affect the build, but should be removed by whoever owns the next commit on this bead.","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:37:26Z"},{"id":2782,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pizld","author":"jemanuel","text":"BeigeCave: closed by 76b5cca9 (github match unbreak + commit message scope) and 8b7cb94a (redirect_allowlist.rs file actually landed — prior commit race dropped the file from the tree). Verified: 'cargo check -p fcp-oauth --lib' compiles, 'cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib redirect_allowlist' 30/30 pass, 'cargo build -p fcp-github --lib' compiles. Sibling bead hds4p was closed by ff27762f but that commit was incomplete; both beads now have working code behind them. The github connector test 'manifest_interface_hash_is_deterministic' fails for an unrelated pre-existing reason (manifest declares 'github.write' capability without listing it in capabilities.required/optional) — not in scope here.","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:25:53Z"}]}
